text: decided on the agent pipeline architecture (3 levels, hard ceiling)
locked the tooling: subagents + headless claude -p for unattended runs
set up AWS + GitHub, secured creds, layered the config
scaffolded the terminal site (Next.js + Tailwind + Geist Mono)
built the click-or-type command engine with tabs
wired the live updates tail you're reading right now
38 tests + coverage gates + privacy guards now block every PR
agent layer scaffolded: lead + 4 workers with scoped tools
registered imsway.dev and shipped to AWS. you're reading this on the live site

built a job requisition agent on watsonx Orchestrate: prompts HR staff for inputs, creates the req in Workday, then chases down approvers via Teams channel alerts - part of an AskHR suite for a sports entertainment industry client

beta-testing a new IBM i developer tooling release: connected to a live IBM i box to transfer, compile, run, and test code directly on the machine. found a couple of bugs, traced the exact fixes, and posted them upstream
built an AIDLC guardrails demo for a utility/energy client: same prompts, run with enforced agent guardrails vs without, to show how rule enforcement changes what the agent produces
hardened this auto-portfolio pipeline so it runs the full loop autonomously while still checking in with me at the right gates

kicked off a new initiative I'm leading: a team of interns building a reusable library of agent modes, skills, rules, and MCP servers that wire an enterprise coding agent into major enterprise products - guardrails and best-practice context baked in - so sales engineers, clients, and internal teams can stand up POCs and pilots fast
built a personal agent for a family member: it scans LinkedIn for roles matching her profile and standards and sends application links, with a beta feature that tailors a resume to each posting

built an objective coding-agent bake-off: runs three AI coding tools in parallel on identical scenarios, each in an isolated repo copy, then scores them on cost, time, tests added, and security with real verification (semgrep, pytest) - turning head-to-head claims into reproducible numbers

wired an AI agent into a live enterprise order-management system over a secured tunnel: ~20 MCP tools plus a catch-all over 1,000+ platform APIs, so it can diagnose a real production bug from the logs, patch it, deploy, and clear stuck orders - cutting diagnosis-to-fix from hours to minutes
mentoring two intern teams through a summer hackathon, coaching them to build their projects with an enterprise AI coding agent

designed and led a full-day mainframe Bobathon for a financial-services company: 40 developers, a live demo where an AI agent modernizes legacy IBM Z green-screens into a modern React UI that pulls live data straight off the mainframe, custom guided labs (COBOL-to-Java, explain legacy code, plan migration), then a hackathon
